examples.title: Pseudocode: Intensity Slicing
examples.description: Divide grayscale range into intervals and assign colors
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IntensitySlicing
INPUT: grayscaleImage, intervalCount
OUTPUT: pseudocolorImage

BEGIN
  FOR EACH interval IN intervalCount
    color = GetColor(interval)
    FOR EACH pixel IN grayscaleImage
      IF pixelIntensity IN intervalRange THEN
        pseudocolorImage[pixel] = color
  RETURN pseudocolorImage
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates intensity slicing, where the grayscale range is divided into intervals and each interval is assigned a specific color.

examples.title: Python: Lookup Table Implementation
examples.description: Implement pseudocolor processing using a lookup table
examples.code:
import numpy as np

# Define a color map (256 RGB values corresponding to gray levels 0-255)
colorMap = np.zeros((256, 3), dtype=np.uint8)
for i in range(256):
  colorMap[i] = [i, i, i]  # Simple grayscale to RGB mapping

# Load input grayscale image
grayscaleImage = np.random.randint(0, 256, size=(256, 256), dtype=np.uint8)

# Create output pseudocolor image
pseudocolorImage = np.zeros((256, 256, 3), dtype=np.uint8)

# Apply lookup table to each pixel
for x in range(256):
  for y in range(256):
    pseudocolorImage[x, y] = colorMap[grayscaleImage[x, y]]
examples.explanation: This Python code implements pseudocolor processing using a lookup table. It defines a color map, loads an input grayscale image, and applies the lookup table to each pixel to create the output pseudocolor image.

examples.title: C: Transformation Function Implementation
examples.description: Implement pseudocolor processing using transformation functions
examples.explanation: This C code implements pseudocolor processing using transformation functions for R, G, B components. It loads an input grayscale image, applies the transformation functions to each pixel, and creates the output pseudocolor image.
